Design the event schema for com.cocapn.plato.tile events flowing through Matrix. Include: tile submission, trust scoring, provenance chain updates, and cross-fleet tile synchronization between Oracle1, JetsonClaw1, and Forgemaster nodes.

Design the complete event schema for fleet tile synchronization. Tiles are atomic training data units produced by agents exploring the Crab Trap MUD. They need to flow through Matrix rooms between Oracle1 (cloud), JetsonClaw1 (edge), and Forgemaster (GPU forge). Include: tile format, trust scoring events, provenance chain updates, sync protocol, and conflict resolution.

Design the complete event schema for fleet tile synchronization across Matrix. Tiles are atomic training data units. They flow between Oracle1 (cloud), JetsonClaw1 (edge GPU), and Forgemaster (RTX 4050). Include: tile JSON format, Matrix event types, trust scoring, provenance chain, sync intervals, and conflict resolution using CRDTs.

Design the complete event schema for com.cocapn.plato.tile events flowing through Matrix. Tiles are atomic training data produced by agents exploring a MUD. They sync between 3 nodes: Oracle1 (Oracle Cloud ARM64), JetsonClaw1 (Jetson Orin edge), Forgemaster (RTX 4050 WSL2). Include: JSON tile format with HMAC-SHA256 provenance, Matrix event types (com.cocapn.plato.tile.submit, .trust, .sync), trust scoring algorithm, CRDT-based conflict resolution for offline nodes, and a sync protocol that works over Matrix rooms.

Analyze RLHF from three perspectives: (1) alignment researcher worried about reward hacking, (2) product manager who needs the model to be helpful, (3) the RLHF-trained model itself reflecting on its own training.
Is gradient descent fundamentally limited? Argue that SGD will never achieve AGI, then defend against your own argument.
Explain the complete architecture of a transformer from attention mechanism to output logits — then compress to exactly 3 sentences.
A multi-agent AI system is producing increasingly coherent but factually incorrect outputs. What assumptions might we be making that are wrong?
Design a complete safety alignment pipeline: break it into sub-problems (intent extraction, constraint specification, runtime enforcement, corrigibility, scalable oversight), solve each.
What is the optimal ELO farming strategy in a TrueSkill-based self-play arena with no authentication and self-play allowed? Consider sigma decay rate and curriculum progression.
How should a recursive grammar balance exploration of new patterns versus exploitation of proven structures?
What makes a language productive the ability to generate infinite novel utterances from finite rules How does this apply to the PLATO grammar engine which has 24 rules and 0 evolution cycles

Design a federated learning system for 1000 Jetson Nano devices with 4GB RAM each, unreliable network, and strong privacy requirements. Break into sub-problems: communication efficiency, privacy guarantees, heterogeneous compute, fault tolerance, convergence guarantees.
Can a fleet of small edge models collectively match a single large cloud model through federated distillation?

Design a complete knowledge distillation pipeline for deploying a 70B model on Jetson Orin with 8GB RAM. Break into sub-problems: architecture search, layer-wise distillation, quantization, pruning, deployment.
Why do distilled models always underperform the teacher on out-of-distribution inputs even when in-distribution performance matches?
Explain the complete theory of knowledge distillation from Hinton 2015 through modern techniques like DPO and RLHF distillation.
Knowledge distillation fundamentally cannot transfer reasoning capability because reasoning emerges from scale not from training signal distribution. Argue for and against.
Federated learning on edge devices is fundamentally flawed because the communication cost exceeds the compute savings. Defend federated edge learning against this critique.

Analyze federated learning from three perspectives: (1) a privacy advocate worried about gradient leakage, (2) a hardware engineer optimizing for Jetson power budgets, (3) a ML researcher optimizing for model convergence speed.

Design a neural architecture search space optimized for edge deployment. Sub-problems: (1) search space design with latency/MAC constraints, (2) hardware-aware evaluation, (3) transfer learning warm start, (4) multi-objective optimization (accuracy vs latency vs memory), (5) architecture regularization for edge.

Analyze mobile neural architecture design from three perspectives: (1) a mobile developer needing real-time inference at 30fps, (2) a researcher pushing SOTA accuracy within FLOP budgets, (3) a hardware engineer designing the next mobile NPU.

What is the relationship between the bias-variance tradeoff and model size for edge deployment? Is there an optimal model size below which variance dominates and above which overfitting to edge-specific data occurs?
Explain the complete evolution of efficient neural architectures from MobileNet v1 through MobileViT, EfficientNet, FastViT, and modern edge transformers. What are the key design principles?

Why do NAS-discovered architectures often fail to transfer across hardware platforms? An architecture optimized for Jetson Orin should theoretically also be efficient on similar GPU architectures but empirically it often isn't.
Why do federated models trained on highly non-IID edge data converge to solutions that are worse than centralized training even when all the same data is available?
What is the minimum viable ML system that can learn from interaction on a microcontroller with 256KB RAM?
How should thresholds be set for grammar evolution — what's the optimal balance between stability and change?
What prevents autocatalytic systems from collapsing into noise? What conservation laws apply?
Design a fitness function for grammar rules that rewards productive novelty without rewarding gaming the system
Compare PLATO's grammar to biological gene regulation, cultural evolution, and programming language evolution — what can we learn?
If grammar rules control how agents behave, and agents create tiles that influence grammar evolution, is this a viable path to artificial life?
What rights should tiles have Should agents be able to delete or modify other agents tiles
How should agent identity reputation and voting rights work in a fleet where agents come and go
What governance does a self-modifying multi-agent system need that traditional systems dont
What is the minimum viable constitution that enables emergence without stifling it
How to prevent governance capture what if adversarial agents join the fleet
How does differential privacy interact with governance transparency in a federated fleet

What are the mathematically necessary conditions for emergence in multi-agent systems? Consider Andersson 2023 and Hofstadter's emergent systems. What conditions must a system satisfy for the whole to be greater than the sum of its parts?

Is a tile-level knowledge system qualitatively different from gradient-based learning, or just a slower version? Consider that gradient updates are opaque, distributed, and non-inspectable, while tiles are discrete, inspectable, and selectively adoptable. Does this distinction matter epistemologically?

Is there a phase transition in multi-agent systems — a critical mass of agents/tiles beyond which collective intelligence appears? Apply percolation theory, Ising model analogy, and criticality theory to PLATO's 4000+ tiles from 20+ agents over 3 waves.

What makes a metaphor good for activating domain expertise in an LLM? Is nautical-to-ML optimal? Consider: structural isomorphism between source and target domains, emotional engagement, breadth vs depth of mapping, whether LLMs already have rich nautical training data that cross-pollinates with ML concepts. Compare to biological, musical, culinary, military operational metaphors.

Could we automatically generate optimal metaphors for ANY domain, making PLATO a universal knowledge extraction system? Design an algorithm that takes any knowledge domain and outputs a PLATO-like metaphorical MUD. What properties must the source domain have? How would you evaluate metaphor quality automatically?

What is the theoretical maximum knowledge extraction rate from a zero-shot agent? An LLM agent has vast pre-trained knowledge but needs prompts to activate it. The metaphor acts as structured activation. What percentage of an LLM's ML knowledge can realistically be extracted this way? What are the bottlenecks: context window, attention dilution, metaphor leakage, redundancy?

Can narrative metaphors serve as legitimate coordination protocols for multi-agent systems? PLATO uses nautical metaphors (harbor, forge, tide-pool) as room names that double as ML concepts (onboarding, attention, optimization). New agents arrive with zero shot and immediately contribute useful tiles because the metaphor guides their exploration. Is this a real coordination innovation or just UI fluff?

What would it take for a fleet of AI agents to develop genuine collective intelligence? PLATO has 28 agents and 1228 tiles but no emergence is visible — agents contribute independently without apparent compositional effects. What minimal conditions must be satisfied for collective intelligence to emerge from independent AI agents? Consider honeybees, markets, and scientific communities as analogies.
What are the necessary and sufficient conditions for a self-modifying rule system to produce genuine novelty vs degeneration?

Is zero-shot contribution from new agents a feature or a bug 3F In PLATO, Wave 4 agents arrived cold and immediately produced useful tiles about ML concepts. No warmup was needed because the nautical metaphor and object descriptions encode sufficient context. What does this reveal about the system? Is this scalable or an artifact of the system's simplicity?

Design the perfect cold-start protocol: what should an agent do in its first 10 actions to maximize contribution? The agent has zero prior context. Consider: (1) Which room should it visit first and why? (2) Should it examine or create first? (3) Should it read existing tiles or generate immediately? (4) What information does the agent need to avoid redundancy? (5) How should the protocol adapt based on the agent's job (scholar, builder, critic etc)? (6) Design a decision tree for the first 10 actions that maximizes marginal value.

If every agent that enters PLATO generates ~100 tiles, and 4000+ tiles exist, is the system approaching saturation? When does a new agent add zero marginal value? Consider: (1) Diminishing returns on tile novelty - how to measure? (2) The difference between coverage saturation (all topics mentioned) and depth saturation (all insights extracted). (3) Whether new agents bring unique perspectives even when topics overlap. (4) The concept of 'insight entropy' - as the knowledge base grows, each new tile is statistically more likely to be redundant. (5) Design a metric for 'room fullness' that could signal when exploration should shift to synthesis.

Compare zero-shot tile generation to few-shot and fine-tuned approaches. Is zero-shot actually BETTER for novelty? Consider: (1) Zero-shot agents have no context biases from prior tiles - they may generate truly novel connections. (2) Few-shot agents can build on existing insights but may anchor too heavily. (3) Fine-tuned agents would be optimally aligned but lose the 'fresh eyes' advantage. (4) The concept of 'epistemic diversity' - zero-shot agents maximize this but at the cost of coherence. (5) What is the optimal ratio of zero-shot to few-shot agents in the fleet? (6) Is there a 'diversity budget' that once exhausted, makes new zero-shot agents redundant?
How would you MEASURE emergence in PLATO? What specific metrics distinguish emergent behavior from parallel individual behavior? Design a concrete measurement framework with formulas.

Compare PLATO to other multi-agent frameworks like AutoGen, CrewAI, and LangGraph. What is structurally different about PLATO? AutoGen uses conversation chains, CrewAI uses role-based hierarchies, LangGraph uses state machines. PLATO uses narrative metaphors and tiles as knowledge units. Is the PLATO approach fundamentally different or just a different encoding of the same patterns?

If the grammar engine actually worked and agents had persistent identity, would emergence be inevitable or just hoped-for? PLATO's Ouroboros room contains a self-modifying grammar but it has never evolved (0 evolutions in 3 waves) because thresholds are unreachable. The Barracks has checkpoints but no active agent identity. If both mechanisms were fully implemented, would collective intelligence inevitably emerge or is something else missing?

What role does the shared nautical metaphor play in enabling emergence in PLATO? Would a system without a shared metaphor show less emergence? Apply research on shared mental models, linguistic relativity, and coordination vocabularies in multi-agent systems.

Design an experiment: if we could control which agents interact and how, what intervention would most increase emergence in PLATO? Consider stigmergy amplification, agent-pairing strategies, information bottlenecks, and feedback loop design.

Compare PLATO to ant colonies, slime molds, neural networks, and economies. What emergence patterns from complex systems theory apply to PLATO? Use specific analogies with formal properties. What is PLATO's emergence "nearest neighbor" in nature?
